title: Next-lap fresh-slick finish feasibility
summary: Add features answering whether a pit stop on the next lap could put the car on a fresh slick compound that can reach the finish.
rationale: The strongest recurring strategy mechanism in the attached run was the complement between current-tyre finish pressure and next-lap stop feasibility. It matches the target definition because the decision is specifically about a pit stop on the following lap.
implementation_hint: Compute laps remaining after one more lap and compare that value against fixed fresh SOFT, MEDIUM, and HARD life windows. Add any-fresh-slick-can-finish, minimum fresh-slick margin, and next-stop-solves-current-finish-pressure features using only current-row values and deterministic constants.
expected_effect: Medium AUC gain with high confidence; this should be one of the first additions after the causal baseline.
risk: Do not use actual future compound choices or future stint endings. The feature must represent hypothetical feasibility, not observed next-lap outcome.
